mappings.rationaleAI: The Dutch East India Company covers its monopoly on Dutch trade in Asia, spice trade, trading posts from the Cape to Japan, and its decline in 1799, directly matching B.2.a on the VOC's establishment of Dutch commercial supremacy and company rule in Java.
The Ming voyages across Southeast Asia provide context for pre-European trade networks in the region, relevant to B.1.a on Portuguese rivalry with the Spanish in the Spice Islands.
The East India Company's rivalry with the Dutch VOC and its role in Malaya and Burma relate to A.1.b on British incursions into Malaya and the assumption of power from sultanates.
